word_list.word: a man of words and not of deeds is like a garden full of weeds
word_list.pronunciation: …ź mňą√¶n  Ćv wňą…úňźdz √¶nd nňĆ…Ďňźt  Ćv dňąiňźdz …™z lňąa…™k …ź …°ňą…Ďňź…Ļd…ôn fňą äl  Ćv wňąiňźdz

senses.definition: used for saying that if a person continuously engages in empty talk without taking productive action, they will not accomplish anything meaningful or valuable
used to emphasize the importance of being efficient, effective, and productive, rather than being overly talkative or boastful

word_list.word: God helps those who help themselves

senses.definition: used to say that individuals who take action and put in effort to achieve their goals and solve their problems are more likely to receive divine assistance or blessings

word_list.word: God never sends mouths but He sends meat
word_list.pronunciation: …°ňą…Ďňźd nňą…õv…ö sňą…õndz mňąa ä√įz bňĆ Ćt hiňź sňą…õndz mňąiňźt

senses.definition: used to imply that people should have faith and trust in God's ability to provide for their needs and to solve their problems
used to imply that people who talk a lot about their plans or achievements may not be as capable as they appear, and that it is important to look at what people do rather than simply what they say

word_list.word: he who is absent, is always in the wrong

senses.definition: used to highlight how people tend to assume guilt or fault when someone is not present to provide their perspective or defend themselves

word_list.word: saying and doing are two things

senses.definition: used to suggest that what people say or promise is not always what they actually do or follow through with
used to emphasize the importance of taking action in the present and avoiding procrastination, as tomorrow is always in the future and becomes today when it arrives

examples.x: If we keep saying that we'll do something tomorrow, we'll never actually get anything done because tomorrow never comes.

senses.definition: used to emphasize the importance of staying cautious and alert, so as not to miss any opportunities

word_list.pronunciation: wňĆ…õn …™n dňąa ät dňąuňź nňąa ät

senses.definition: used to advise that  when one is uncertain about what to do, it may be better to refrain from taking any action at all

word_list.word: many words will not fill a bushel
word_list.pronunciation: mňą…õni wňą…úňźdz w…™l nňĆ…Ďňźt fňą…™l …ź bňą ä É…ôl

senses.definition: used to emphasize the need for action rather than mere talk or empty promises, as using many words does not necessarily lead to to achieving one's goals or solving a problem

examples.x: When my friend kept talking about his plans to start a business, I reminded him that many words will not fill a bushel, and that he needed to start taking action.

word_list.word: good words without deeds are (nothing but|meer|) rushes and reeds
word_list.pronunciation: …°ňą äd wňą…úňźdz w…™√įňĆa ät dňąiňźdz …Ďňź…Ļ nňą Ćőł…™Ňč bňĆ Ćt mňą…™…Ļ …Ļňą Ć ÉŠĶĽz √¶nd …Ļňąiňźdz

senses.definition: used to imply that words or promises are meaningless without actions to back them up

examples.x: When my friend kept making promises but failing to follow through, I reminded him that good words without deeds are rushes and reeds.

word_list.word: the road to hell is paved with good intentions
word_list.pronunciation: √į…ô …Ļňąo äd t…ô hňą…õl …™z pňąe…™vd w…™√į …°ňą äd …™ntňą…õn É…ônz

senses.definition: used to imply that good intentions, plans, and promises are only meaningful if they are followed through with action

examples.x: The politician acknowledged that the road to hell is paved with good intentions, and that the best way to win the trust and support of voters was to deliver on campaign promises and take concrete actions to address issues.
